mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in real retail sales signal strengthening consumer spending that drives corporate revenue growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year rank IC of RSAFS changes versus 20-day forward returns is below 0.03, or the hit rate of positive returns when RSAFS rises month-over-month is not significantly above 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% it signals labor market weakening that reduces aggregate demand and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of UNRATE threshold breach compared to 20-day forward returns is non-negative over a 3-year window, or the average return after breaches is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising consumer price inflation and unchanged or rising federal funds rate compresses real interest rates downward, reducing the equity risk premium and lowering S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The spread between FEDFUNDS and trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation (real rate) shows absolute rank IC below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window when predicting 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: During a low interest rate environment defined by FEDFUNDS below 2%, rising housing starts indicate robust residential investment that boosts GDP growth and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 2%, the correlation between HOUST changes and 60-day SPX returns is not positive or the rank IC over any 2-year window is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden acceleration in consumer price inflation above a threshold of 330 (scaled index) erodes real purchasing power and raises the probability of hawkish Fed action, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of CPIAUCSL exceeding 330 versus 5-day forward returns is positive over any 2-year window, or the average excess return after threshold breaches is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising nonfarm payrolls and low unemployment indicate an overheated labor market that forces the Fed to tighten, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as rate expectations rise.
failure_condition: The joint signal (PAYEMS increase > 200k and UNRATE below 4%) fails to produce average negative 5-day returns significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals a robust expansion in aggregate economic output, which drives corporate earnings higher and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The rank IC of GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth with 60-day forward SPX returns is below 0.03 over any 5-year rolling window, or the hit rate of positive returns following positive GDP prints is not above 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In a manufacturing expansion regime where industrial production rises above its 12-month moving average, accelerating factory output signals strong economic momentum that lifts equity markets over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO exceeds its 12-month MA, the average 20-day SPX return is not positive and statistically significant (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), or rolling 3-year win rate is below 55%.
